commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: Often washing: Syr. betilarth, i.e, diligently or carefully; Gr. πυγμη̃, zealously; Heb. caph el cabh, i.e, hand to hand, namely, by constant rubbing, as they do who wish to cleanse defiled hands.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Often washing Some would have the Greek to signify unless they wash up to the elbows, but I think without sufficient grounds. (Witham)
